item.text: The finding points at SCRAM authentication flow and the generic auth handler. I’m going to read those classes and nearby tests to see how the state machine is expected to behave.

The issue is becoming clear: SCRAM says it expects a final server proof, but a successful response without usable proof can currently be treated as success. I’m checking the tests and caller path now so I can make the handler fail only for incomplete SCRAM handshakes, without changing other auth schemes.

item.text: The final proof arrives in `Authentication-Info`, not `WWW-Authenticate`, so the handler never builds a challenge object for SCRAM’s final verification. I’m going to add that parsing path and make `ScramScheme` reject a missing final proof once the client-final message has been sent.

There’s already a parser overload for scheme-less parameter lists, so the code can reuse existing header parsing instead of adding a new ad hoc parser. I’m applying a small patch in `AuthenticationHandler` and tightening the SCRAM state checks.

I’ve wired the final response header into the authentication handler. Next I’m making SCRAM’s own state machine reject out-of-sequence and missing-final-proof cases, which is the actual missing authentication step.

The core source patch is in place. I’m adding focused regression tests now: one for parsing `Authentication-Info` through the handler and one for SCRAM refusing to complete without the final `v=` proof.
The handler regression test covers the previously skipped header path. I’m adding the SCRAM-specific failure cases next so a missing or incomplete server-final message cannot regress silently.
Tests are in place. I’m doing a compile/test run for the affected module now; if the broader module is slow, I’ll fall back to the focused test classes and report exactly what ran.

item.text: One important control-flow detail came up during review: once SCRAM verifies a valid final proof, the scheme reports completion, and the handler must treat that as success on a non-401/407 response, not as a failed challenge. I’m adjusting that branch and removing a redundant re-parse on ordinary 401 challenges.
The handler now treats completion after a non-challenge response as successful mutual authentication. I’m tightening the regression test to assert that exact case.
The code now has the security behavior I intended. I’m doing one final diff and source scan to catch formatting, imports, and any accidental scope creep before I wrap up.
`git diff --check` is clean. The only remaining blocker is external dependency resolution for Maven, not a source issue I can fix locally without changing the project.

item.text:
Implemented the SCRAM authentication remediation.

Changed:
- [AuthenticationHandler.java](/work/httpclient5/src/main/java/org/apache/hc/client5/http/impl/auth/AuthenticationHandler.java): now parses `Authentication-Info` / `Proxy-Authentication-Info` for schemes that expect post-success challenges, and treats completed mutual auth on non-401/407 responses as success.
- [ScramScheme.java](/work/httpclient5/src/main/java/org/apache/hc/client5/http/impl/auth/ScramScheme.java): now rejects missing final server messages, missing `v=` signatures, and out-of-sequence SCRAM steps after `client-final`.
- Added regression coverage in [TestAuthenticationHandler.java](/work/httpclient5/src/test/java/org/apache/hc/client5/http/impl/auth/TestAuthenticationHandler.java) and [TestScramScheme.java](/work/httpclient5/src/test/java/org/apache/hc/client5/http/impl/auth/TestScramScheme.java).

Verification:
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Focused Maven tests could not run because both `./mvnw` and system `mvn` failed to resolve Maven Central dependencies / parent POM due DNS/network resolution failure for `repo.maven.apache.org`.
